---
title: "ATUS Part1"
author: "Alisha"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Data Exploration
```{r}
cps <- read.csv("data/atuscps_2024.dat")
act_sum <- read.csv("data/atussum_2024.dat")
resp <- read.csv("data/atusresp_2024.dat")
```

```{r}
colnames(cps)
```

```{r}
colnames(act_sum)
```
```{r}
colnames(resp)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# --- 1. Keep only essential columns ---
act_small <- act_sum %>%
  select(
    TUCASEID,
    TEAGE,
    TESEX,
    TELFS,
    TESCHENR,
    TESCHLVL,
    starts_with("t")
  )

cps_small <- cps %>%
  select(TUCASEID, PEEDUCA)

# --- 2. Merge ---
df <- act_small %>%
  left_join(cps_small, by = "TUCASEID")

# --- 3. Helper: sum ATUS activity columns by prefix ---
sum_prefix <- function(data, prefix) {
  cols <- grep(paste0("^", prefix), names(data), value = TRUE)
  if (length(cols) == 0) return(rep(0, nrow(data)))
  rowSums(data[, cols, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
}

# --- 4. Create broad time-use categories (minutes per day) ---
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    sleep_min  = sum_prefix(., "t0101"),
    work_min   = sum_prefix(., "t0501"),
    educ_min   = sum_prefix(., "t0601") + sum_prefix(., "t0603") + sum_prefix(., "t0604"),
    sports_min = sum_prefix(., "t1301"),
    social_min = sum_prefix(., "t1201") + sum_prefix(., "t1202"),
    tv_min     = sum_prefix(., "t120303"),
    travel_min = sum_prefix(., "t18")
  )

# --- 5. Restrict age range if desired ---
# You can change this to 20-24, 20-29, etc.
df2 <- df %>%
  filter(TEAGE >= 20, TEAGE <= 24)

# --- 6. Create proxy groups ---
# PEEDUCA cutpoint:
# 43+ = bachelor's or higher in CPS education coding
df2 <- df2 %>%
  mutate(
    ba_plus = PEEDUCA >= 43,

    group = case_when(
      TESCHENR == 1 & TESCHLVL == 2 & !ba_plus ~ "Undergrad",
      TESCHENR == 1 & TESCHLVL == 2 &  ba_plus ~ "Graduate student proxy",
      TESCHENR == 2 & TELFS %in% c(1, 2) & ba_plus ~ "Employed new-grad proxy",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(group))

# --- 7. Keep only the variables needed for analysis ---
analysis_df <- df2 %>%
  select(
    TUCASEID, group, TEAGE, TESEX,
    sleep_min, work_min, educ_min, sports_min, social_min, tv_min, travel_min
  )

# --- 8. Compare average time use by group ---
summary_table <- analysis_df %>%
  group_by(group) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    sleep_hrs   = mean(sleep_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    work_hrs    = mean(work_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    educ_hrs    = mean(educ_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    sports_hrs  = mean(sports_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    social_hrs  = mean(social_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    tv_hrs      = mean(tv_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    travel_hrs  = mean(travel_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60
  )

print(summary_table)
```

```{r}
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

plot_df <- summary_table %>%
  select(-n) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -group,
    names_to = "category",
    values_to = "hours"
  )

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = category, y = hours, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Average Time Use by Group in 2024",
    x = "Time Category",
    y = "Average Hours per Day",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```

```{r}
sex_counts <- analysis_df %>%
  mutate(
    sex = case_when(
      TESEX == 1 ~ "Male",
      TESEX == 2 ~ "Female",
      TRUE ~ "Other/Unknown"
    )
  ) %>%
  count(sex)

ggplot(sex_counts, aes(x = sex, y = n, fill = sex)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Sex Distribution in Final Dataset",
    x = "Sex",
    y = "Number of Respondents"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
group_counts <- analysis_df %>%
  count(group)

ggplot(group_counts, aes(x = group, y = n, fill = group)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Respondents in Each Group",
    x = "Group",
    y = "Number of Respondents"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1)
  )
```


```{r}
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    personal_care_min = sum_prefix(., "t01"),
    household_min     = sum_prefix(., "t02"),
    caring_hh_min     = sum_prefix(., "t03"),
    caring_nonhh_min  = sum_prefix(., "t04"),
    work_min          = sum_prefix(., "t05"),
    education_min     = sum_prefix(., "t06"),
    consumer_min      = sum_prefix(., "t07"),
    services_min      = sum_prefix(., "t08") + sum_prefix(., "t09") + sum_prefix(., "t10"),
    eating_min        = sum_prefix(., "t11"),
    leisure_min       = sum_prefix(., "t12"),
    sports_min        = sum_prefix(., "t13"),
    religion_min      = sum_prefix(., "t14"),
    volunteer_min     = sum_prefix(., "t15"),
    phone_min         = sum_prefix(., "t16"),
    travel_min        = sum_prefix(., "t18")
  )

# --- 5. Restrict age range if desired ---
# You can change this to 20-24, 20-29, etc.
df2 <- df %>%
  filter(TEAGE >= 20, TEAGE <= 24)

# --- 6. Create proxy groups ---
# PEEDUCA cutpoint:
# 43+ = bachelor's or higher in CPS education coding
df2 <- df2 %>%
  mutate(
    ba_plus = PEEDUCA >= 43,

    group = case_when(
      TESCHENR == 1 & TESCHLVL == 2 & !ba_plus ~ "Undergrad",
      TESCHENR == 1 & TESCHLVL == 2 &  ba_plus ~ "Graduate student proxy",
      TESCHENR == 2 & TELFS %in% c(1, 2) & ba_plus ~ "Employed new-grad proxy",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(group))

# --- 7. Keep only the variables needed for analysis ---
analysis_df <- df2 %>%
  select(
    TUCASEID, group, TEAGE, TESEX,
    sleep_min, work_min, educ_min, sports_min, social_min, tv_min, travel_min
  )

# --- 8. Compare average time use by group ---
summary_table <- analysis_df %>%
  group_by(group) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    personal_care_hrs = mean(personal_care_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    household_hrs     = mean(household_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    caring_hh_hrs     = mean(caring_hh_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    caring_nonhh_hrs  = mean(caring_nonhh_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    work_hrs          = mean(work_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    education_hrs     = mean(education_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    consumer_hrs      = mean(consumer_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    services_hrs      = mean(services_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    eating_hrs        = mean(eating_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    leisure_hrs       = mean(leisure_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    sports_hrs        = mean(sports_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    religion_hrs      = mean(religion_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    volunteer_hrs     = mean(volunteer_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    phone_hrs         = mean(phone_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    travel_hrs        = mean(travel_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60
  )
library(ggplot2)

plot_df <- summary_table %>%
  select(-n) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -group,
    names_to = "category",
    values_to = "hours"
  )

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = category, y = hours, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Average Time Use by Group in 2024",
    x = "Time Category",
    y = "Average Hours per Day",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```
